title: ranunculus parviflorus subsp. chius
intro: Ranunculus parviflorus subsp. chius, also known as lesser celandine or pilewort, is a flowering plant that belongs to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it useful for treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne, as well as wounds and infections. The health benefits of lesser celandine include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a potential natural remedy for various health issues. Lesser celandine can be prepared as an herbal remedy through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, and is also used in some traditional recipes as a culinary ingredient.
